name: BTC 4h Value Recovery Scale-In v1
edge_mechanism: price flushes away from short-term value, reclaims value, confirmation improves across bars, and exposure is built in stages instead of one-shot timing
thesis: The cycle 84 primary lane is BTC/4h, and the new packet explicitly still wants scale-in around value recovery. The last 5 cycles say sparse 4h rescue logic is dead, so this version must stay dense: flush, reclaim, improved confirmation, then staged entry. BTC 4h is the best ownership lane because it is transition-capable enough to resume after recovery without being as anti-trend-flat as ETH or as late-entry-biased as TAO.
invalidation_condition: If BTC 4h value-recovery sequences still fail to clear moderate screen density or staged adds mostly average into failed bounces, the thesis fails.

pre_submit_reasoning.market_behavior_targeted: Price flushes below short-term value, reclaims it, confirms the reclaim across bars, and resumes away from the recovery zone.
pre_submit_reasoning.why_edge_should_exist: Transition-capable majors often punish one-shot bounce timing but reward building into improving confirmation. This is denser and more tradeable than rare delayed continuation or deep reclaim hero entries.
pre_submit_reasoning.asset_timeframe_fit: BTC 4h is the best fit because it is the live primary lane, mixed enough to print recurring recovery sequences, and less likely than ETH 4h to collapse into pure chop-only rotation.
pre_submit_reasoning.likely_failure_mode: Staged entries may still add into weak mean-reversion bounces if the regime slips back into flat chop without follow-through.
pre_submit_reasoning.first_refinement_path: If density is healthy but expectancy is weak, reduce second-add size and move the first partial closer before changing the trigger grammar.
pre_submit_reasoning.expected_outcome_pattern: Repeated recovery attempts, many modest first-target wins, and fewer larger moves when recovery becomes true continuation.
pre_submit_reasoning.what_would_count_as_success: BTC 4h clears density and staged recovery materially outperforms one-shot recovery timing.
pre_submit_reasoning.what_would_count_as_failure: 0 trades, sub-15 trades, or repeated scale-ins that behave like averaging losers.

density_sanity_check.event_sparsity: acceptable because flush-and-recovery sequences recur in mixed chop/transition

validation_basket.why_included: Best-fit primary lane for staged value recovery.
validation_basket.continue_if: BTC 4h shows enough value-recovery sequences with scale-in improving net expectancy.
validation_basket.rotate_away_if: BTC 4h staged entries still mostly build into failed bounces.
validation_basket.expected_if_right: Moderate participation with several recovery continuations.

validation_basket.why_included: Checks whether the same staged recovery survives on a similar but flatter major.
validation_basket.continue_if: ETH 4h retains a smaller but tradable staged recovery behavior.
validation_basket.rotate_away_if: ETH 4h is too flat or too shallow to monetize.
validation_basket.expected_if_right: Cleaner, smaller recovery continuations than BTC.

validation_basket.why_included: Tests whether the same mechanism gains density on 1h without collapsing into the intraday noise that has already failed repeatedly.
validation_basket.continue_if: BTC 1h increases count while preserving actual recovery structure.
validation_basket.rotate_away_if: BTC 1h reintroduces the same noisy churn seen in earlier failed intraday cycles.
validation_basket.expected_if_right: Higher count, smaller recovery continuations than BTC 4h.

long: Initial 60% size on first close back above VWAP
Add 40% only if next bar closes >= EMA_20 and low stays >= VWAP - 0.06 * ATR_14
short: Initial 60% size on first close back below VWAP
Add 40% only if next bar closes <= EMA_20 and high stays <= VWAP + 0.06 * ATR_14

long: Stop loss: 0.78 * ATR_14 below blended entry
short: Stop loss: 0.78 * ATR_14 above blended entry
